content.full_text:
Cali orIlia 
air Political Practices CommissioIL 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO,95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
107 SOUTH BROADWAY 
• •• 
SUITE 2140A 
••• 
LOS ANGELES, 90012 
January 24. 1977 
Ms. Janet Stubbs 
City Clerk 
City of Southgate 
Southgate. California 90280 
Dear Ms. Stubbs: 
This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on January 1,4, 1977, 
concerning the filing requirements of Government Code Section 84206. 
You stated that a Southgate City Councilman was a candidate for a .iudicial 
office in 1976 in the June primary and November run-off elections. 
You stated 
further that he was elected to the city council in March of 1976 and filed with 
your office a post-election campaign statement with a closing date of April 
30, 1976. 
You asked whether the councilman must file in January, 1977 an 
off year report covering the period from May 1, 1976 through December 31, 1976. 
I advised that the councilman is not required to file an off-year report 
in January 1977. 
Government Code Section 84206 states that elected officers 
who file campaign statements in connection with any election held within the 
specified reporting period are not required to file an off-year statement 
for that period. 
Therefore, the next off-year statement which the city 
councilman must file will be due anytime during July 1977, covering the per-
iod from the closing date of his last post-election statement, filed in 
connection with the judicial campaign, through June 30, 1977. 
If you or the councilman have any questions relative to the above, please 
contact me. 
Sincerely, 
Nan Emmerson 
Special Compliance Representative 
NHE:lh 
SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 
•

sections.question: Whether a Southgate City Councilman who was elected to the city council in March 1976 and filed a post-election campaign statement with a closing date of April 30, 1976, and was a candidate for judicial office in June primary and November run-off elections in 1976, must file an off-year report in January 1977 covering the period from May 1, 1976 through December 31, 1976.
sections.conclusion: The councilman is not required to file an off-year report in January 1977. Government Code Section 84206 states that elected officers who file campaign statements in connection with any election held within the specified reporting period are not required to file an off-year statement for that period. Therefore, the next off-year statement which the city councilman must file will be due anytime during July 1977, covering the period from the closing date of his last post-election statement, filed in connection with the judicial campaign, through June 30, 1977.
sections.question_synthetic: Must an elected city councilman file an off-year campaign statement in January 1977 for the period May 1 through December 31, 1976, given that he filed a post-election statement in April 1976 and was also a judicial candidate in 1976?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No off-year report is required in January 1977 because the councilman filed campaign statements in connection with elections held within the reporting period. The next required off-year report is due in July 1977, covering the period from April 30, 1976 through June 30, 1977.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: FPPC response letter confirming prior telephone advice. Clear question and conclusion sections based on document structure. Addresses Government Code Section 84206 filing requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a Southgate City Councilman who was elected to the city council in March 1976 and filed a post-election campaign statement with a closing date of April 30, 1976, and was a candidate for judicial office in June primary and November run-off elections in 1976, must file an off-year report in January 1977 covering the period from May 1, 1976 through December 31, 1976.

CONCLUSION: The councilman is not required to file an off-year report in January 1977. Government Code Section 84206 states that elected officers who file campaign statements in connection with any election held within the specified reporting period are not required to file an off-year statement for that period. Therefore, the next off-year statement which the city councilman must file will be due anytime during July 1977, covering the period from the closing date of his last post-election statement, filed in connection with the judicial campaign, through June 30, 1977.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises a Southgate City Clerk that a city councilman is not required to file an off-year campaign statement in January 1977, as Government Code Section 84206 exempts those who filed statements in connection with elections held during the reporting period. The next required off-year report will be due in July 1977.
